Article Title: 2 murders in Bengaluru: 26-year-old man found dead on pavement; brawl turns fatal in JC Nagar
Article Text:
AI-generated representational image.

Brawl ends in murder





BENGALURU: A 26-year-old-man was found stabbed to death on the pavements of Balagere Main Road, Varthur, in the wee hours of Saturday.The victim has been identified as M Dala Ram from Rajasthan. He was residing with his cousin in Varthur. "He had come to the city four days ago, looking for a job. We are questioning his cousins and others about the incident," a police officer said.A brawl between three friends ended on a fatal note as two stabbed the other to death in JC Nagar Saturday. The victim is Vijay alias Layout, 25, of RT Nagar.Police arrested the accused - Uday Kiran and Vinod Kumar, both in their late 20s - on charges of stabbing Vijay to death. "Vijay had allegedly abused Uday's girlfriend, making Uday angry. The accused persons fought with Vijay near a bar around 11am, Saturday and stabbed him to death," a cop said.
Article Summary: A 26-year-old man was found stabbed to death on the pavements of Balagere Main Road, Varthur, in the wee hours of Saturday. The victim has been identified as M Dala Ram from Rajasthan. He had come to the city four days ago, looking for a job.

Article Title: West Bengal polls: TMC lodges complaint against EC officer for breaching Model Code of Conduct
Article Text:
Image: IANS

Watch "Mamata K Ghar Jake Usko Harana Hai," Shah Urges Voters Ahead Of Elections "Mamata K Ghar Jake Usko Harana Hai," Shah Urges Voters Ahead Of Elections

KOLKATA: A row erupted Saturday after a viral video allegedly showed an EC official for Panihati constituency on Kolkata's outskirts preparing BJP campaign material, prompting TMC to demand his removal and legal action before elections.Trinamool lodged a complaint with the state's chief electoral officer (CEO) against sector officer Atanu Chakraborty, accusing him of violating norms and compromising neutrality required of election staff. The complaint cited a video circulating on social media that purportedly shows Chakraborty arranging BJP flags and pamphlets.In a letter to the CEO, TMC MP Derek O'Brien alleged that the officer engaged in activities linked to BJP, breaching the model code of conduct and other provisions governing the conduct of election personnel. Seeking urgent intervention, party functionaries urged that Chakraborty be stripped of all poll duties and face strict legal proceedings, as "his conduct is a serious threat to free and fair elections".
Article Summary: Trinamool lodged a complaint with the state's chief electoral officer (CEO) against sector officer Atanu Chakraborty. TMC MP Derek O'Brien alleged that the officer engaged in activities linked to BJP, breaching the model code of conduct and other provisions governing the conduct of election personnel.

Article Title: West Bengal polls: EC suspends 4 police officers after TMC workers gatecrash Amit Shah’s Bhabanipur roadshow
Article Text:
Watch "Mamata K Ghar Jake Usko Harana Hai," Shah Urges Voters Ahead Of Elections "Mamata K Ghar Jake Usko Harana Hai," Shah Urges Voters Ahead Of Elections

Poll What is your perception of political rallies and their role in elections? Essential for voter engagement Often leads to chaos

NEW DELHI: The Election Commission on Saturday directed West Bengal chief secretary to suspend four Kolkata Police officials for "failing" to maintain law and order during BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari's nomination filing in Bhabanipur constituency for the upcoming assembly polls.In a circular to the West Bengal chief secretary, the poll panel said that the direction is based on a recommendation from state electoral officer Manoj Kumar Agarwal."The commission has agreed to the proposal and directs that the aforementioned police officers be suspended and disciplinary proceedings be initiated against them immediately," the circular read.The missive also directed action against Kolkata Police's Deputy Commissioner (DC-II), South Division, Siddhartha Dutta; Officer-in-Charge of Alipore Police Station Priyankar Chakraborty; Additional OC Chandi Charan Banerjee; and Sergeant Saurabh Chatterjee.Additionally, the EC also asked the state authorities to ensure that its directions are implemented without delay and that a compliance report be submitted by 11 am on Monday.The poll panel also sought an urgent proposal from the state government to fill up the vacant posts, including that of DC-II, South Division, and the officer-in-charge positions at Alipore Police Station.This comes after TMC workers waved party flags during BJP's roadshow before Adhikari's nomination filing on Thursday. Union minister Amit Shah had also accompanied Adhikari for the filing of the nomination papers on April 2 in the seat in south Kolkata, where the leader of the opposition in the outgoing Assembly is pitted against incumbent chief minister Mamata Banerjee As the convoy approached Kalighat, barely a few hundred metres from Banerjee's residence, Trinamool Congress workers and supporters gathered on the roadside waving green-and-white party flags, shouting 'Joy Bangla' and 'Mamata Banerjee Zindabad' slogans.However, the situation turned tense when the BJP roadshow and the TMC protest came face to face near Kalighat.For several minutes, supporters of the two rival camps stood barely a few metres apart, hurling slogans at each other.A thick cordon of police personnel formed a human wall between the two groups to prevent any clash, with officers pushing the rival supporters back on either side of the road.The face-off brought traffic to a halt and heightened tension in the area before the BJP convoy moved ahead.
Article Summary: The Election Commission on Saturday directed West Bengal chief secretary to suspend four Kolkata Police officials for "failing" to maintain law and order. This comes after BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari's nomination filing in Bhabanipur constituency for the upcoming assembly polls.

Article Title: Assam assembly elections 2026: Crorepati candidates including CM in fray but richest name will surprise you
Article Text:
Watch Second Term For Himanta Or Surprise In Store? Political Analyst K Bharatram Decodes Assam Elections Second Term For Himanta Or Surprise In Store? Political Analyst K Bharatram Decodes Assam Elections

Name Party name Constituency Total assets (in Rs) Rahul Roy INC Hailakandi 261 crore+ Mohammed Badruddin Ajmal AIUDF Binnakandi 226 crore+ Jayanta Khaund INC Rongonadi 67 crore+ Sewli Mohilary Bodoland Peoples Front Kokrajhar (ST) 63 crore+ Rojy Ahmed AITC Hajo-Sualkuchi (SC) 54 crore+ Nandigiri Bhuyan IND Sissiborgaon 47 crore+ Himanta Biswa Sarma BJP Jalukbari 35 crore+ Ashok Singhal BJP Dhekiajuli 34 crore+ Shri Naba Kumar Doley BJP Dhakuakhana (ST) 33 crore+ Aminul Haque Laskar INC Sonai 30 crore+

As Assam gears up for the 2026 assembly elections, a glance at candidate affidavits shows a strong presence of crorepatis in the fray — including chief minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. However, while the CM figures among the wealthiest contenders, he is far from the top of the list, with several candidates declaring significantly higher assets.Topping the chart is Congress candidate Rahul Roy with assets exceeding Rs 261 crore, followed by AIUDF leader Badruddin Ajmal at over Rs 226 crore. The list also includes names from across party lines, underscoring the dominance of high-net-worth candidates in the state’s electoral landscape.Here's the list of top 10 richest candidates:Elections in Assam are scheduled for April 9, with counting of votes set for May 4. Considered a crucial contest, the polls are expected to witness several high-profile battles that could significantly influence the state’s political trajectory.Central to the election is a closely watched face-off between CM Himanta Biswa Sarma and Congress leader Gaurav Gogoi, seen as a key test of whether the BJP can secure a majority on its own.
Article Summary: A glance at candidate affidavits shows a strong presence of crorepatis in the fray. Congress candidate Rahul Roy tops the chart with assets exceeding Rs 261 crore. The list also includes names from across party lines, underscoring the dominance of high-net-worth candidates.

Article Title: Khushbaz Singh Jatana Accident: Stuck on highway, Punjab Congress leader Khushbaz Singh Jatana, driver killed as truck slams Defender
Article Text:
By: Manoj Dhaka

Poll What do you think should be prioritized more regarding road safety? Strict enforcement of traffic rules Better road infrastructure and awareness





SONIPAT: Punjab Congress functionary Khushbaz Singh Jatana and his driver were killed, while his gunman was critically injured after a speeding truck rammed into their stationary Land Rover Defender SUV on the Kundli-Manesar-Palwal (KMP) Expressway near Sonipat on Thursday evening.Jatana had contested the 2017 and 2022 Punjab assembly elections from Talwandi Sabo as a Congress candidate, but lost both times.According to police, the accident took place at 6.15pm near the Jakhauli toll plaza. Jatana was travelling from Delhi to Punjab in a Land Rover Defender SUV when the vehicle reportedly developed a tyre puncture.Police said the SUV had been pulled over to the roadside and a local mechanic had been called to replace the tyre. While the driver and mechanic were changing the wheel, Jatana and his gunman were standing near the vehicle when a speeding truck allegedly hit the SUV from behind.The impact was severe, and Jatana and the driver died on the spot, police said. The gunman sustained critical injuries, while the mechanic was also hurt.The injured were rushed to Sonipat civil hospital, from where the gunman was referred to Rohtak for advanced treatment.The mechanic is undergoing treatment.Rai police station SHO Shetty Malik said the truck driver fled the spot with the vehicle. Police have identified the truck number, registered a case and launched a manhunt. CCTV footage from nearby areas is also being scanned. The bodies have been sent for postmortem.
Article Summary: Punjab Congress functionary Khushbaz Singh Jatana and his driver were killed. His gunman was critically injured after a speeding truck rammed into their stationary Land Rover Defender SUV. Jatana had contested the 2017 and 2022 Punjab assembly elections from Talwandi Sabo as a Congress candidate.
